ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Reshmi Kebab; closest match is chicken kebab (FOODON_03315091). Reshmi Kebab is a specific Mughlai variant with cream, cashew paste, and yogurt marinade.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to Reshmi Kebab. A0FJP (cooked chicken meat) is the closest defensible anchor. Reshmi Kebab includes dairy marinade components not captured by this code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Reshmi Kebab or a sufficiently close prepared chicken dish variant.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Reshmi Kebab is approximately 150g, equivalent to 2–3 skewered kebab pieces, as commonly served as a starter or snack portion.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Reshmi Kebab is not listed as a discrete entry in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN data for grilled chicken preparations and standard Mughlai marinade components (yogurt, cream, cashew paste). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, cooked, roasted; ID 05013) was used as a secondary cross-reference for core macros. Values represent a composite estimate for the finished dish per 100g.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Reshmi Kebab. GI estimated at 25 (low) based on analogous high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled chicken preparations from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish contains only 3.2g carbohydrate per 100g; the high protein (16.5g) and fat (14.5g) content further attenuate postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 150g serving = (25 × 4.8g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 1.2, classified as very low.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (320mg/100g) and saturated fat from cream and butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Reshmi Kebab to occasional consumption; a 150g serving delivers approximately 480mg sodium (about 21% of the 2,300mg daily limit), and the saturated fat from cream and ghee may contribute to arterial stiffness — reducing cream and butter in preparation lowers both sodium and saturated fat load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (3.2g/100g) and low estimated glycemic index (~25)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Reshmi Kebab is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions; its very low carbohydrate content and low GI (~25) produce minimal postprandial glucose excursion, though the high caloric density from fat (14.5g/100g) warrants portion control to avoid excess energy intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cream, butter/ghee, and cashew paste; cholesterol at 85mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: People with cardiovascular disease should consume Reshmi Kebab in moderation; the saturated fat (~5.2g/100g) and cholesterol (85mg/100g) from dairy and ghee can raise LDL cholesterol, and substituting low-fat yogurt for cream and using minimal ghee basting would meaningfully reduce the cardiovascular risk profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High-fat dairy content (cream, yogurt) may trigger inflammation in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Chicken provides lean protein beneficial for muscle maintenance in rheumatoid arthritis; however, individuals sensitive to high-fat dairy should monitor their response to the cream and yogurt marinade and consider substituting with low-fat alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Reshmi Kebab is a classic Mughlai appetizer originating from North India, celebrated for its distinctively silky ('reshmi' means 'silk' in Hindi/Urdu) texture and mild, creamy flavour profile. The dish is prepared by marinating finely minced or chunked chicken in a rich blend of full-fat yogurt, fresh cream, cashew nut paste, lemon juice, white pepper, and green chili paste, then skewering and grilling the pieces in a tandoor or over charcoal, with regular basting of butter or ghee to maintain succulence. Unlike the more robustly spiced Seekh Kebab or Tandoori Chicken, Reshmi Kebab is intentionally mild and aromatic, making it a popular choice for those who prefer subtle heat. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides approximately 210 kcal, 16.5g protein, 14.5g fat (including ~5.2g saturated fat from dairy and ghee), and only 3.2g carbohydrate, placing it in the high-protein, moderate-fat, low-carbohydrate category. Its very low glycemic index (~25) makes it suitable for people managing Type 2 Diabetes, though the sodium content (320mg/100g) and saturated fat warrant moderation for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. A standard serving is 150g (2–3 pieces), delivering approximately 315 kcal and 480mg sodium. The dish is halal, gluten-free, and contains dairy and tree nut (cashew) allergens.
common_user_queries: What does reshmi mean in food?

confidence_reason: Reshmi Kebab is not listed as a discrete entry in any single approved primary source. Nutritional values are adapted from ICMR-NIN grilled chicken and Mughlai marinade component data, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for core macros. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on comparable high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled chicken preparations. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Saturated fat estimated at 5.2g/100g based on ICMR-NIN and USDA component data for cream, butter/ghee, and cashew paste proportions typical of Reshmi Kebab; not directly measured. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation and moisture data for this specific Mughlai preparation are not available in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 3. GI value of 25 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Reshmi Kebab was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2024. 4. Portions input contained three identical duplicate entries (150g); deduplicated to a single default serving of 150g. 5. Halal status assumed based on standard preparation with chicken and no pork-derived ingredients; individual preparation may vary. 6. Cashew nut paste is a tree-nut allergen; dairy (yogurt, cream, ghee) is a milk allergen — both must be declared.
